design_philosophy.core_loop: Match rows and columns of cookies by rotating grid lines (Rubik's Cube mechanic); clear matching sets; manage incoming cookie flow from top and right edges
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure and spatial overflow; player must clear cookies faster than the grid fills, preventing game over condition
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a puzzle-solving expert; satisfying pattern recognition and spatial manipulation; competitive disruption in VS Mode
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rubik's Cube-inspired rotation mechanic differentiates it from typical match-3 games (Tetris, Dr. Mario). Emphasizes line-rotation strategy over dropping/swapping. Wild card (Yoshi head) acts as flexible solution tool. Heavy focus on two-player competitive disruption as equal emphasis to single-player progression

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Game difficulty escalates through successive levels with progressively complex grid arrangements. Players navigate difficulty through skill mastery of rotation mechanic and spatial planning

progression.unlock_mechanism: Action Mode progresses through successive levels of increasing complexity. Puzzle Mode (SNES version) presents predefined grids with move limits
replayability_hooks: VS Mode two-player competitive gameplay with disruption mechanics
Increasing difficulty levels encourage skill refinement
Multiplayer competitive disruption creates dynamic challenge

world_building_techniques: Yoshi IP branding (character appearance as wild card)
Mario universe integration (published by Nintendo, Yoshi/Mario character licensing)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Yoshi's Cookie (NES) article
